codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully hardens delivery-loop status projection and refresh behavior. Key improvements include: (1) Adaptive polling intervals in deliveryLoopStatusQueryOptions that adjust based on delivery loop state - 15s for active phases, 30s for blocked/waiting, 60s for terminal, 120s for initial load; (2) Enhanced shouldRefreshDeliveryLoopStatusFromThreadPatch to detect PR link changes (prNumber, prUrl) and shell status changes while ignoring non-status field changes; (3) Comprehensive test coverage with 27 tests validating all VAL-UI assertions.

VAL-UI-001.evidence: Adaptive polling with 15s interval for active phases (planning, implementing, review_gate, ci_gate, ui_gate, babysitting). Tests verify polling behavior for each state.

VAL-UI-002.evidence: Blocked/waiting states (blocked, awaiting_pr_link) use 30s polling interval - more frequent than terminal but less aggressive than active phases.

VAL-UI-003.evidence: Terminal states (done, stopped, terminated_pr_closed, terminated_pr_merged) use 60s polling - reduced frequency appropriate for terminal states.

VAL-UI-004.evidence: shouldRefreshDeliveryLoopStatusFromThreadPatch detects PR link changes via prNumber and prUrl field detection in shell patches. Tests verify refresh triggers on PR link visibility changes.

VAL-UI-005.evidence: Function checks for shell.status field changes and refetch arrays containing 'shell' to trigger refresh. Non-status field changes (logs, artifacts, etc.) are filtered out. Tests validate both material and ignored patch types.

VAL-UI-006.evidence: Awaiting-pr-link and babysitting states are covered: awaiting_pr_link maps to 30s blocked polling, babysitting maps to 15s active polling. Tests verify correct interval selection.

VAL-CROSS-007.evidence: Combination of server-side state from getDeliveryLoopStatusAction, adaptive client polling, and precise patch-based invalidation ensures API and browser state remain consistent throughout lifecycle.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill 'delivery-loop-runtime-worker' referenced in the transcript does not exist at .factory/skills/delivery-loop-runtime-worker/SKILL.md. The transcript shows the worker invoked this skill but it may have been renamed or consolidated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript line: 'Skill: { skill: "delivery-loop-runtime-worker" }' - File not found at expected location.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The query helper pattern in apps/www/src/queries/server-action-helpers.ts provides getServerActionQueryOptions which is used consistently for server-action-based queries with TanStack Query. This is a well-established pattern for type-safe server action queries.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: delivery-loop-status-queries.ts uses getServerActionQueryOptions pattern with proper queryKey management and staleTime/refetchInterval configuration.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Delivery loop status tests are split across three files following a logical separation: delivery-loop-status.test.ts (runtime helpers), get-delivery-loop-status.test.ts (server action integration), delivery-loop-status-queries.test.ts (query behavior). This is a clean test organization pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Three test files with 23, 11, and 4 tests respectively covering distinct layers of the stack.

summary: Feature m5-harden-ui-status-projection successfully implements adaptive polling intervals for delivery-loop status queries, precise patch-based refresh triggers, and comprehensive test coverage. All VAL-UI-001..006 and VAL-CROSS-007 contract assertions are satisfied. The implementation is well-structured, follows existing codebase patterns, and all 27 tests pass. No blocking issues found.
